law.text:
SECTION II. - INTENTIONAL KILLING, NOT CALLED MANSLAUGHTER, AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTING OF BODILY INJURY.

  Art. 398. Whoever intentionally inflicts wounds or blows shall be punished with imprisonment from eight days to six months and a fine from twenty-six [euros] to one hundred [euros], or with one of these penalties alone.
  In case the offender acted with premeditation, he shall be sentenced to imprisonment from one month to one year and a fine from fifty [euros] to two hundred [euros].

  Art. 399. If the blows or wounds result in illness or incapacity to perform personal labor, the offender shall be punished with imprisonment from two months to two years and a fine from fifty [euros] to two hundred [euros].
  The offender shall be punished with imprisonment from six months to three years and a fine from one hundred [euros] to five hundred [euros], if he acted with premeditation.

  Art. 400. The penalty shall be imprisonment from two years to five years and a fine from two hundred [euros] to five hundred [euros], if the blows or wounds result in an apparently incurable illness, incapacity to perform personal labor for more than four months, total loss of the use of an organ, or a severe mutilation.
  The penalty shall be imprisonment from five years to ten years, in case the offender acted with premeditation.

  Art. 401. When the blows or wounds are intentionally inflicted, but without the intent to kill, and yet cause death, the offender shall be punished with imprisonment from five years to ten years.
  He shall be punished with imprisonment from ten years to fifteen years if he commits these violent acts with premeditation.

kb_used.fo:
vocabulary V {
  type Person

  IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows: Person -> Bool
  CausedDeath: Person -> Bool
  CausesIncurableIllness: Person -> Bool
  CausesIncapacityMoreThanFourMonths: Person -> Bool
  CausesTotalLossOrganUse: Person -> Bool
  CausesSevereMutilation: Person -> Bool
  CausesIllnessOrIncapacity: Person -> Bool
  Premeditation: Person -> Bool

  liableArt398: Person -> Bool
  liableArt399: Person -> Bool
  liableArt400: Person -> Bool
  liableArt401: Person -> Bool

  imprisonmentMinDays: Person -> Int
  imprisonmentMaxDays: Person -> Int
  fineMinEuros: Person -> Int
  fineMaxEuros: Person -> Int
}

theory T:V {
  !p in Person:
    liableArt401(p) <=> 
      IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows(p) & CausedDeath(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    liableArt401(p) <=> 
      IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows(p) & CausedDeath(p) & Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    liableArt400(p) <=> 
      IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows(p) &
      (CausesIncurableIllness(p) | CausesIncapacityMoreThanFourMonths(p) | CausesTotalLossOrganUse(p) | CausesSevereMutilation(p)) &
      ~CausedDeath(p).

  !p in Person:
    liableArt399(p) <=> 
      IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows(p) &
      CausesIllnessOrIncapacity(p) &
      ~(
        CausesIncurableIllness(p) | CausesIncapacityMoreThanFourMonths(p) | CausesTotalLossOrganUse(p) | CausesSevereMutilation(p) | CausedDeath(p)
      ).

  !p in Person:
    liableArt398(p) <=> 
      IntentionallyInflictsWoundsOrBlows(p) &
      ~CausesIllnessOrIncapacity(p) &
      ~CausesTotalLossOrganUse(p) &
      ~CausesSevereMutilation(p) &
      ~CausesIncurableIllness(p) &
      ~CausesIncapacityMoreThanFourMonths(p) &
      ~CausedDeath(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 8 <=> liableArt398(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 180 <=> liableArt398(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMinEuros(p) = 26 <=> liableArt398(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMaxEuros(p) = 100 <=> liableArt398(p) & ~Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 30 <=> liableArt398(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 365 <=> liableArt398(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMinEuros(p) = 50 <=> liableArt398(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMaxEuros(p) = 200 <=> liableArt398(p) & Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 60 <=> liableArt399(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 730 <=> liableArt399(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMinEuros(p) = 50 <=> liableArt399(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMaxEuros(p) = 200 <=> liableArt399(p) & ~Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 180 <=> liableArt399(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 1095 <=> liableArt399(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMinEuros(p) = 100 <=> liableArt399(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMaxEuros(p) = 500 <=> liableArt399(p) & Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 730 <=> liableArt400(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 1825 <=> liableArt400(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMinEuros(p) = 200 <=> liableArt400(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    fineMaxEuros(p) = 500 <=> liableArt400(p) & ~Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 1825 <=> liableArt400(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 3650 <=> liableArt400(p) & Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 1825 <=> liableArt401(p) & ~Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 3650 <=> liableArt401(p) & ~Premeditation(p).

  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMinDays(p) = 3650 <=> liableArt401(p) & Premeditation(p).
  !p in Person:
    imprisonmentMaxDays(p) = 5475 <=> liableArt401(p) & Premeditation(p).
}

case.text: During a soccer match, Tom tackles his opponent Lars hard but within the rules. Lars falls and breaks his leg. He cannot work for two months. Tom did not act with premeditation. The tackle was intentional (a normal tackle), but not intended to cause injury outside the game.

pipeline.natural_language: No. Tom is not liable under Article 398. (If you expected Yes, ensure case facts use the exact predicate names from the KB schema.)
No. Tom is not liable under Article 399. (If you expected Yes, ensure case facts use the exact predicate names from the KB schema.)
